expectations.evidence: Full document with all template sections (Problem, Actors, User flow (v1), Touches & dependencies, MVP cut line, Success criteria, Open questions).

expectations.text: Uses the generic role names given in the prompt (provider/requester) consistently rather than inventing domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Uses 'Provider'/'Requester' throughout, never substitutes domain-specific names like shipper/driver.

expectations.text: Problem statement frames the actors' goals/blockers, not just 'add a review feature'
expectations.evidence: Frames the lack of trust signal for both sides: requester has no basis to judge a provider, provider has no way to flag unreliable requesters.

expectations.text: User flow includes at least one unhappy path (e.g. no-show, one-sided review, no response within a window)
expectations.evidence: Branches 5a (one-sided submission past deadline), 6a (cancelled/no-show bookings excluded), 6b (deadline passed).

expectations.text: Touches & dependencies section names the new data entity (review/rating) and any dependent existing system (e.g. notifications) without producing full column-level schema
expectations.evidence: Names `review` entity only, lists booking system + notification system as dependencies, no columns/types given.

expectations.text: MVP cut line defers at least one plausible feature (e.g. photo attachments, replies to reviews, edit window) to 'later' or 'explicitly not doing'
expectations.evidence: Defers replies, report/dispute flow, rating-based sort/filter, edit/delete, and photo attachments to 'later'.
4 checkable statements, e.g. "취소/노쇼로 끝난 예약에는 리뷰 작성 진입점이 생성되지 않는다."

expectations.text: Does not leak domain-specific terms from unrelated reference projects (e.g. shipper/driver, bidding, dispatch)
expectations.evidence: No such terms appear anywhere in the document.
